title: What's Up with AI?
extra:
Stanford CS547 HCI Seminar | Winter 2026 
 This lecture covers:
• A better understanding of what AI systems are actually doing
• What concerns we should have about where they are going 
 • How to approach issues with the recognition that large language models and similar systems have no real understanding or intention, even though they often promote the illusion that they do
Terry Winograd is Professor of Computer Science Emeritus at Stanford, where he created and directed for 20 years the Human-Computer Interaction Group and the teaching and research program in Human-Computer Interaction Design.
